messages.content:
The Federalist Papers are a collection of essays written by Alexander Hamilton, John Jay, and James Madison and published pseudonymously in the Daily Advertiser and other New York newspapers in 1787-88. Though the authorship of most of the essays is certain, that of one is obscure: for instance, while No. 12, "The Utility of the Union in Respect to revenue," was surely penned by Hamilton, No. 52, "The House of Representatives," may have been written by either Hamilton or Madison. The difficulty of determining No. 52's author arises not from a lack of evidence but from too much contradictory evidence.
Based on the text, which choice best explains why it is challenging to confidently assign authorship of the essay "The House of Representatives”?
D. The evidence that would indisputably prove that either Hamilton or Madison was the sole author of "The House of Representatives" has been
lost.
C. It is most likely that "The House of Representatives" was the product of a collaborative effort between Hamilton and Madison.
B. Evidence shows that two versions of "The House of Representatives," one authored by Hamilton and another authored by Madison, appeared in different New York newspapers.
A. Support can be found for a
strong case that either Hamilton or Madison was the sole author of "The House of Representatives."
A. Support can be found for a strong case that either Hamilton or Madison was the sole author of "The House of Representatives."

"Lines Written in Early Spring" is a 1798 poem by William Wordsworth. In the poem, the speaker describes having contradictory feelings while experiencing the sights and sounds of a spring day:_
Which quotation from "Lines Written in Early Spring" most effectively illustrates the claim?
A. "Through primrose-tufts, in that sweet bower, / The periwinkle trail'd its wreathes; / And 'tis my faith that every flower/Enjoys a the air it breathes."
C. "The birds around me hopp'd and play'd: / Their thoughts I cannot measure, / But the least motion which they made, / It seem'd a thrill of pleasure."
"I heard a thousand blended notes. / While in a grove I [sat] reclines. / In that sweet mood when pleasant thoughts/ Bring sad thoughts to the mind".
B. "The budding twigs spread out their fan, / To catch the breezy air: / And I must think, do all I can,/ That there was pleasure there."

The quotation that most effectively illustrates the speaker having contradictory feelings while experiencing the sights and sounds of a spring day is:

"I heard a thousand blended notes, / While in a grove I [sat] reclined, / In that sweet mood when pleasant thoughts / Bring sad thoughts to the mind."

This quotation indicates the presence of a mix of sensations—pleasant auditory experiences of nature's harmony juxtaposed with the emergence of sad thoughts. This contrast captures the essence of feeling contradictory emotions: being in a setting that evokes both happiness and sorrow.

In the early 1970s, Albert Popa took up graffiti art, spraying his work onto what was at the time an unconventional surface: concrete. Albert's son David has chosen an unusual canvas for his new art project, Fractured. In this remarkable work, the artist draws charcoal faces onto fragmented ice floes in Finland, creating the visual effect of a face slowly fracturing. Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
B. Indeed,
A. However,
D. Likewise,
C. Second,

D. Likewise,

The word "Likewise" creates a logical transition here as it connects two instances of artists choosing unconventional surfaces for their artwork. Albert Popa used concrete while his son David is using fragmented ice floes, implying a similarity in their approach to selecting unique canvases for their art.

Before the first railroads were built in Australia, engineers warned against using multiple track widths across the continent. _regional officials couldn't agree on a single-width system. Thus, railways in Victoria, like the Victorian Goldfields Railway, were made using wide tracks, while those in New South Wales were built with narrow tracks. Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
A. In other words,
D. Nevertheless,
B. Similarly,
C. For this reason,

D. Nevertheless,

The word "Nevertheless" is the most logical transition in this context because it introduces a contrast. The engineers warned against using multiple track widths, suggesting that a singular, standardized track width should be used. However, despite these warnings, regional disagreements led to the adoption of different track widths, which is contrary to the initial advice. "Nevertheless" effectively communicates this turn of events from the recommendation to the actual outcome.

In a 2022 analysis of 200 terms, researchers found a broad pattern of valence-dependent mutation in which negative adjectives saw a faster rate of cognate replacement-the rate at which a word will be replaced over time with a noncognate form-than other words. the adjective "illegal" would be expected to mutate faster than the noun "debt." Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
A. Thus,
D. Moreover,
B. Meanwhile,
C. However,

A. Thus,

The word "Thus" is the most logical transition because it indicates a conclusion or result that follows from the findings mentioned in the preceding sentence. The analysis found that negative adjectives mutate faster; therefore, it would be logical to predict that "illegal," being a negative adjective, would mutate faster than "debt," which is not an adjective. "Thus" introduces this expected outcome based on the established pattern of valence-dependent mutation.

Legislators in Botswana are elected via a first-past-the-post electoral system. In this winner-take-all voting system, the seat is awarded to the candidate who receives not a majority but a plurality (i.e., greatest number) of votes. if several popular candidates are vying for the same seat, it is possible for a candidate to win an election despite receiving less than 50% of the votes. Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
B. However,
C. Moreover,
A. Accordingly,
Similarly,

A. Accordingly,

"Accordingly" is the most logical transition, as it connects the explanation of the first-past-the-post electoral system with the potential consequence of that system. The system is defined by the candidate with the most votes winning, which can result in a candidate being elected without a majority—this is a natural outcome or implication of that system, making "Accordingly" the appropriate transition.

The world's first airplane, the 1903 Wright Flyer, was not very aerodynamic, as evidenced by its low lift-to-drag ratio of just 8.3:1. The F-4 Phantom II jet, like most modern aircraft, is far more aerodynamic.___________its lift-to-drag ratio is more than twice that of the Wright Flyer.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
C. Nevertheless,
A. Specifically,
D. In conclusion,
B. However,

A. Specifically,

The word "Specifically" is the most logical transition here because it signals that more detailed information is about to be provided regarding the aerodynamic quality of the F-4 Phantom II jet, which has already been stated to be far more aerodynamic than the Wright Flyer. This transition leads into the specific data point comparing the lift-to-drag ratios of the two aircraft, further illustrating the improvement in aerodynamics over time.

The following text is from Guy de Maupassant's 1884 short story "A Recollection,” from the collection Guy de Maupassant Short Stories (translated by Albert M.C. McMaster et al. in 1903). The narrator is on a walk in the countryside outside of Paris, France.
I walked slowly beneath the young leaves, drinking in the air, fragrant with the odor of young buds and sap. I sauntered along, forgeful of musty papers, of the offices, of my chief, my colleagues, my documents, and thinking of the good things that were sure to come to me, of all the veiled unknown contained in the future. A thousand recollections of childhood came over me, awakened by these country odors, and I walked along, permeated with the fragrant, living enchantment, the emotional enchantment of the woods warmed by the sun of June. Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
A. The narrator is using the quiet of the countryside to help him think about how projects at work could be advantageous for his career.
The narrator's natural surroundings help him escape his work concerns by prompting hopes about the future and memories of youth.
C. After a brief visit to his childhood home, the narrator decides to leave his job and return there to live permanently.
B. The narrator forgets to address pressing work issues because the smells of the countryside distract him with thoughts of his youth.
B. The narrator's natural surroundings help him escape his work concerns by prompting hopes about the future and memories of youth.

In her 1983 book The Managed Heart: Commercialization of Human Feelids sociologist Arlie Russell Hochschild first explored at length her conception of a "sociology of emotions"—the idea that the various cultural and ideological frameworks a person has internalized (class, gender, political affiliation, etc.)_ _each emotional reaction that person has within a
situation.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
C. underlie
D. has been underlying
B. is underlying
A. underlies

A. underlies

The choice "underlies" completes the sentence so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English. It provides a singular verb that agrees with the singular subject "idea" and offers the present tense, which is consistent with the tense used throughout the sentence. The full sentence, therefore, reads:

"...the idea that the various cultural and ideological frameworks a person has internalized (class, gender, political affiliation, etc.) underlies each emotional reaction that person has within a situation."

The National Heritage Fellowship was created to publicly. exceptional folk and traditional artists in the United States. In 2012, the fellowship was given to the klezmer musician and composer Andy Statman to celebrate his lifetime contributions to
the arts.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
D. startle
C. convey
A. recognize
B. familiarize

A. recognize

The word "recognize" completes the text with the most logical and precise word because the National Heritage Fellowship is intended to honor and acknowledge exceptional artists for their contributions. None of the other options fit the context of an award being given for lifetime achievements. The full sentence would then read:

"The National Heritage Fellowship was created to publicly recognize exceptional folk and traditional artists in the United States."

Woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted using a carpet weaving technique passed down by the artist's Turkish grandmother, so lush and tactilely inviting that you are tempted to reach out and touch them. Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
C. when she creates her topological tapestries, Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou makes them
A. the topological tapestries of Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou are
B. the Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou creates topological tapestries that are
D. Alexandra Kehayoglou is an Argentine artist whose topological tapestries are

B. the Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou creates topological tapestries that are

Completing the sentence with this choice conforms to the conventions of Standard English by ensuring that the sentence has a clear subject and a verb, which are necessary for a complete sentence. The full sentence would read:

"The Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou creates topological tapestries that are woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted using a carpet weaving technique passed down by the artist's Turkish grandmother, so lush and tactilely inviting that you are tempted to reach out and touch them."

Each year, the Nobel Prize in Literature is given to an author who has, in the words of its founder Alfred Nobel, "produced the most outstanding work in an idealistic_ 2020, for instance, judges recognized Louise Glück for her unmistakable poetic voice that with austere beauty makes individual existence universal."
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
A. direction": in
B. direction." In
D. direction" in
C. direction" and, in

B. direction." In

This choice is correct because it properly places the period inside the quotation marks and capitalizes the independent clause that follows. According to Standard English conventions, periods and commas should be placed inside quotation marks. The full sentence would correctly read:

"Each year, the Nobel Prize in Literature is given to an author who has, in the words of its founder Alfred Nobel, 'produced the most outstanding work in an idealistic direction.' In 2020, for instance, judges recognized Louise Glück for her unmistakable poetic voice that with austere beauty makes individual existence universal."

The Study on Global Ageing and Adult Health (SAGE) examines trends in aging among 66,000 people in multiple countries unfolding over many years. As is true of most longitudinal studies, this need for years of data collection results in high costs. By contrast, a relatively straighforward sociology study, such as one that is merely trying to identify the average age at which people in a given city first become parents, may not need a large budget because Which choice most logically completes the text?
A. Longitudinal methods are probably suitable for the sociology study.
B. it would be easy for SAGE researchers to add questions to their aging study.
D. the sociology study can be| C. 66,000 people is more than done well without years of
data.enough for SAGE to findtrends in aging.

C. the sociology study can be done well without years of data.

This choice best completes the text because it contrasts the high costs associated with longitudinal studies, which require many years of data collection, with the lower costs of a sociology study that does not require a long-term approach. The text is making a point about the cost implications of different types of research methodologies, and this choice provides a logical reason explaining why the sociology study would be less expensive, namely, it does not require extended data collection over time.
